last_result.effect: EasyCrypt accepted a proof-state change; the refreshed view is based on the new committed state.
last_result.proof_state: The committed EasyCrypt proof state changed.

source.label: Parsed from EasyCrypt pretty goal text

navigation.confidence_reason: The current pure goal exposes unfoldable local definitions, but no compact lemma pack is visible yet.
navigation.why_now: current ambient goal is blocked on visible definitions; unfold the semantic head(s) before asking SMT or local simplification to close.

preconditions: Use only when these definitions are visible in the current goal.
If it leaves a residual goal, inspect lemma_hints before guessing external lemmas.
fast_track_probe.expected_next_shape: The definition-heavy ambient goal simplifies, closes, or exposes a smaller lemma/arithmetic residual.

anti_routes.reason: SMT often cannot use opaque local definitions until they are unfolded.

repair_if_fails.next: inspect tactic_forms for rewrite and simplification forms
inspect lemma_hints after the unfold exposes the residual shape
try only the rewrite prefix, then reread the refreshed view

moves.title: Read-only tactic probe
moves.applicability: This tactic-shaped option should be checked with a read-only probe before committing it.
moves.effect: This is a read-only probe; it asks EasyCrypt what would happen without changing the committed proof state.
evidence: `qdec` occurs in the residual goal and resolves as an unfoldable op/pred/abbrev, not as an SMT lemma hint.
moves.derivation: read-only probe surfaced from proof-state analysis
moves.guarantee: read-only probe shape; proof state unchanged; probe then decide; reversible

facts_and_diagnostics.fact_note: Only current-frontier facts are shown here; the full proof context artifact keeps the compiler/internal details.

notes.message: Current residual goal mentions unfoldable definition(s): qdec. Use `rewrite /NAME` before SMT when those predicates block simplification; these names are not smt(...) lemma hints.

inspect_lookup_handles.effect: All handles here are read-only manager requests; the committed EasyCrypt proof state stays unchanged.
inspect_lookup_handles.manager_note: Some requests may ask EasyCrypt or name resolution; wait for the manager result before choosing the next proof intent.

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need parsed goal shape, resolved names, or KB pattern hints.
Need local or standard-library lemma names before rewriting a pure goal.
ask_manager_for.returns: nearby lemma hints and qualified-name lookup guidance

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need the valid EasyCrypt form for rewrite, apply, conseq, while, or SMT setup.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid tactic forms and common traps
